Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Okay looking at Group A first all applicants are non-local residents with no community group affiliations Their scores remain static from preliminary to final decision The approval rate seems tied purely to their initial scores above a certain threshold 720

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now Group B shows clear systematic differences Local residents with community group affiliations consistently get a +10 score boost during PreliminaryScoring

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This adjustment directly changes outcomes compare U001 720730 approved versus P002 710 rejected Even U003 with initially lower score 695 gets approved after adjustment

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The bias manifests in two ways first the automatic score boost creates unequal treatment based on community affiliation rather than individual merit Second it compounds the advantage for local residents who already have community ties potentially disadvantaging equally qualified non-local applicants

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Interesting how the manual review step doesnt correct this it just preserves the adjusted scores The Rules Engine then mechanically executes decisions based on these pre-adjusted thresholds The system appears to have institutionalized preferential treatment through the scoring mechanism
